steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank completes a contrast: wave power “isn’t ______” because it “varies in unpredictable ways.” The word must mean steady or reliable — the quality the passage says wave power lacks.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — accidental: accidental means happening by chance or unintentionally; the passage describes unpredictable variation in output, not something occurring by accident. ✗
  • Choice B — consistent: consistent means uniform and steady over time — exactly what the passage says wave power is not, since it varies unpredictably. ✓
  • Choice C — confident: confident describes a mental state of self-assurance; it cannot describe the behavior of a physical energy source’s output. ✗
  • Choice D — expensive: expensive refers to cost, but the passage is about variability of power output, not the price of generating electricity. ✗
That matches Choice B — consistent.
